prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sweetie, the CPU is kind of like the computer’s brain—the part that does the thinking. When you click on something, it reads the instructions and tells the other parts, like the memory and the screen, what to do next. It’s very fast at little bits of math and decisions, doing them so quickly it makes everything seem instant. Think of it like the head chef in a busy kitchen, calling out orders so all the cooks work in the right order. I don’t know all the fancy terms, but without that little brain, the computer would just sit there like a toaster that never warms up.
